title: Antirrhinum mollissimum
taxonomy: Antirrhinum mollissimum is classified within the family Plantaginaceae. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships with other members of the family. The genus Antirrhinum is further distinguished within the tribe Antirrhineae. The tribe Antirrhineae is a part of the subfamily Plantaginoideae. This classification system is based on the shared characteristics and genetic similarities among these plant species.

Its leaves and stems are rich in antioxidants and flavonoids, which have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the medicinal properties and uses of Antirrhinum mollissimum.

intro: Antirrhinum mollissimum, also known as Downy Snapdragon, is a perennial plant species in the family Plantaginaceae, characterized by its velvety leaves and showy, bell-shaped flowers. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which may aid in treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Antirrhinum mollissimum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoid glycosides, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat coughs, sore throats, and digestive issues.
